notes: HBM structure is valid, but the price path and broad recognition require 4B-watch at the same time.

company_name: SK하이닉스 record profit, HBM visibility, and capital return
notes: Record HBM-led profit and capital return strengthen visibility, but the rerated price path still needs 4B-watch validation.
Catch-up shipping can be Stage 2, but Green needs customer qualification, yield, and volume shipment evidence.
AMD HBM4 and foundry MOU can support catch-up visibility, but MOU is not volume shipment or margin evidence.
Even a memory catch-up story can be blocked by labor, execution, foundry yield, or talent retention risk.

company_name: Micron consumer memory exit and AI supply reallocation
notes: Consumer memory exit can indicate AI/HBM allocation pressure, but it must convert into mix, OPM, and EPS evidence.
AI storage can make NAND look structural for a time, but a 20x stock path needs 4B/cycle reversal review.
AI CAPEX is visible in equipment and packaging guidance, but CAPEX-cycle risk still keeps it Watch-to-Green.

company_name: Broadcom custom AI ASIC hyperscaler demand
notes: Hyperscaler custom ASIC revenue can be Stage 2 or better only when capacity, named customer, margin, and repeat revenue are visible.

company_name: Custom AI ASIC customer concentration and margin watch
notes: Custom ASIC visibility must be capped when revenue depends on a narrow customer base, lower custom-chip margin, or startup customer credit.
CoWoS capacity expanded sharply but remains a bottleneck; both facts must be tracked together.
Optical networking/PCB bottleneck becomes stronger in Loop 10 because lead time expanded from weeks to months.

company_name: Cisco AI networking orders and switching guidance
notes: AI networking orders and guidance can support Stage 2, but legacy mix, restructuring cost, and order-to-margin conversion remain checks.

company_name: Tower Semiconductor AI data-center photonics deal
notes: A photonics chip contract can be Stage 2 when amount and delivery are known; Green needs revenue recognition, margin, and customer diversity.
AI server revenue and rack shipments can pass Stage 2, but margin and working-capital quality decide conviction.
Cooling can be a structural data-center bottleneck, but acquisition multiple and accretion timing remain RedTeam fields.

company_name: CoreWeave OpenAI contract with leverage risk
notes: A huge AI cloud contract can be Stage 2 visibility, but FCF, debt, and customer concentration block Green.

company_name: CoreWeave expanded OpenAI contract concentration watch
notes: Expanded OpenAI contract raises visibility, but customer concentration and GPU/debt economics still block Green.
Circular AI financing can inflate demand visibility; it must be RedTeam evidence until clean FCF and customer diversity are proven.

company_name: Cerebras AI accelerator IPO event premium
notes: AI accelerator pure-play demand can be Stage 2 watch evidence, but IPO surge and ecosystem/customer risk keep it event premium.
AI server growth cannot overcome auditor resignation, filing delay, or internal-control trust break.
CXL, glass substrate, and neuromorphic tags remain Watch/Red until adoption, validation, production, and revenue exist.
Related-stock exposure is not evidence. Customer validation, tape-out, mass production, and revenue must come first.
Crowded AI CAPEX evidence is a 4B warning overlay and must not create positive Green score by itself.
